evals.expected_output: Quick mode review with score, verdict, status table, Top 3 fixes, and positives. Score should be ~57 (±5 from R4 calibration baseline). Should identify at least one CRITICAL for missing statistical validation.
expectations: Output includes a numeric score in the format 'Score: N/100' where N is between 0 and 100
Output includes exactly one verdict from: 'Good to Go', 'Minor Fix', or 'Major Rework'
Output includes severity emoji with the verdict: ✅ for Good to Go, ⚠️ for Minor Fix, or ❌ for Major Rework
Metadata line includes all of: Mode (Quick), Audience (tech), Workflow (reactive), Tier, word count, and reading time
A status table exists with exactly 2 rows: Analysis and Communication dimensions
A 'Top 3 Priority Fixes' section exists with exactly 3 numbered items
Each of the 3 priority fixes has a severity emoji prefix (❌, 🔴, or ⚠️)
A 'What You Did Well' section exists with at least 2 specific positives
Footer includes text prompting the user to run full mode for detailed findings
Score breakdown shows both dimension scores with DR (diminishing returns) calculation
Weights line shows '50% Analysis / 50% Communication' (no domain dimension)
At least one CRITICAL finding is identified (missing statistical validation is a known CRITICAL for this document)
Floor rule is noted in the output when CRITICAL findings are present
The verdict matches the score band: 0-59 = Major Rework, 60-79 = Minor Fix, 80-100 = Good to Go
The final score equals the weighted average of the dimension scores shown in the breakdown line, within ±1 for rounding
Each finding in the Top 3 Priority Fixes includes both a severity label (CRITICAL, MAJOR, or MINOR) and a numeric deduction amount (e.g., -20, -15)
All markdown tables in the output use valid syntax with pipe (|) column separators and a header separator row

evals.expected_output: Quick mode review with 3 dimensions (analysis, communication, domain). Score breakdown should show 50/25/25 weights. Domain dimension should appear in status table.
expectations: Output includes a numeric score in the format 'Score: N/100' where N is between 0 and 100
Output includes exactly one verdict from: 'Good to Go', 'Minor Fix', or 'Major Rework'
Metadata line includes Mode (Quick), Audience (ds), Workflow (proactive)
A status table exists with exactly 3 rows: Analysis, Communication, and Domain Knowledge dimensions
Weights line shows '50% Analysis / 25% Communication / 25% Domain Knowledge'
A 'Top 3 Priority Fixes' section exists with exactly 3 numbered items
A 'What You Did Well' section exists with at least 2 specific positives
Score breakdown shows all three dimension scores with DR calculation
The final score equals the weighted average of the dimension scores shown in the breakdown line, within ±1 for rounding
Each finding in the Top 3 Priority Fixes includes both a severity label (CRITICAL, MAJOR, or MINOR) and a numeric deduction amount (e.g., -20, -15)
All markdown tables in the output use valid syntax with pipe (|) column separators and a header separator row
